title: Podman container created in cgroupv2 is unable to start when host rebooted in cgroupv1
body:
### Issue Description

We have found out an issue in podman application. When we create a podman container in a host with cgroupv2 hierarchy then it works normally without any issue but when we reboot our host with cgroupv1 hierarchy and tries to start that container then it does not get started and raise below given error :-

"Error: unable to start container "32358ee8c18a076c944f73294888df38d2cfbcf1f20e1bf4154b1ceb4fc17cdd": cgroup namespace is not supported with cgroup v1 and systemd mode"

Therefore, this error should not come whether the container is created in cgroupv1 or cgroupv2 container should start normally.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Check if host in cgroupv2, for cgroupv2 type will be cgroup2fs and for cgroupv1 type will be tmpfs
 stat -f /sys/fs/cgroup/

2.  Create the container
sudo podman create --name testnode-v2 --hostname testnode-v2 --entrypoint="/usr/sbin/init" --memory 8g --systemd true oraclelinux:8

3. Start the container
sudo podman start testnode-v2

4. Reboot the host in cgroupv1 hierarchy
sudo grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0 systemd.legacy_systemd_cgroup_controller"

5. Start the container  testnode-v2 which was created in cgroupv2 hierarchy
[my_vm ~]$ sudo podman start testnode-v2
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment variable `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
Error: unable to start container "32358ee8c18a076c944f73294888df38d2cfbcf1f20e1bf4154b1ceb4fc17cdd": cgroup namespace is not supported with cgroup v1 and systemd mode



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Podman uses the cgroup namespace by default when cgroup v2 is detected and that is part of the container configuration.

The cgroup namespace breaks systemd in a container on cgroup v1, so the alternative would be to ignore the setting by loosening the container configuration (dropping the namespace could leak information into the container that was not available before).  I am not sure this should be done automatically.
I agree, if you change the underlying cgroups, you need to recreate the containers, this is not something we will fix.  People should just move to cgroupV2 and be done with it.

Hi @rhatdan @giuseppe , is there a way to migrate containers which were created in cgroupv2, to a cgroupv1 system? Also, I think containers should work even if the underlying OS settings are changed. Is there a problem if the cgroup settings of the system is detected at container start, and the container is able to come up accordingly?  Why do the cgroup settings have to be persisted as part of the container state ?

> Hi @rhatdan @giuseppe , is there a way to migrate containers which were created in cgroupv2, to a cgroupv1 system? Also, I think containers should work even if the underlying OS settings are changed.

no, unfortunately you cannot update the namespaces used by a container.

> Is there a problem if the cgroup settings of the system is detected at container start, and the container is able to come up accordingly? Why do the cgroup settings have to be persisted as part of the container state ?

yes, I think it is a problem if we loosen the configuration.  It would work on cgroupv1 if the container was created with `--cgroupns=host` so to override the different default on cgroup v2

Do you consider portability as a requirement in the forward direction at least ? i.e Should containers created on a cgroupv1 system, work when the system is later booted in cgroupv2?  I would think that should be supported at least, if not the backward direction (v2->v1), do you agree ?
I don't think the upstream Podman team makes any support promise for this, but I agree it would be nice.
